gaps.description: All six Articles of the Bill of Rights (Scope of Enterprise, Prohibited Activities, Core Identity, Human Authority Reservation, Amendment Protocol, Interpretation Rules) are marked '[To be defined by human principal]'. The BoR status is 'scaffold — awaiting human authorship'. Without a substantive BoR, the DIO has no defined scope, no prohibited activities, no core identity, and no codified reservation of human authority. Arbiter (#190) verifies the BoR at every scope query, but the document it verifies is substantively empty. Nomos (#200) evaluates Action Proposals against MSP constraints, but the MSP itself is strictly subordinate to a BoR that has no content. The entire two-gate authorization architecture rests on an empty constitutional document.

The MSP Rules section explicitly states: 'The bootstrap seed contains no rules. The DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation until Senate publishes substantive rules.' The seed was published on 2026-04-10 and remains unchanged as of 2026-05-03. Nomos (#200) is the second compliance gate that evaluates every Action Proposal against MSP rules and genome constraints; with no rules defined, this gate is effectively open. The Senate (#220) has not published any substantive rules via the PEM → Legislator → review → Rollout pipeline.

The MSP Genome References section contains no entries: '(none at seed)'. Genome documents are meant to extend the MSP with organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints. Every organ listed in spine_state (Senate, Nomos, Arbiter, Radiant, Minder, Glia, Vigil, Axon, etc.) operates without formalized genome constraints. The MSP §Genome References specifies that these live under 'genome/' in the MSP directory.

Spine state transitions show a consistent pattern: all 28 organs simultaneously transition from ALIVE to DEGRADED (reason: 'missed_pong'), then approximately 30 seconds later all simultaneously recover to ALIVE (reason: 'pong_recovered'). This cycle repeats continuously (observed across multiple cycles in the snapshot from 18:07 to 18:14 UTC on 2026-05-03). Governance-critical organs — Senate (#220), Nomos (#200), Arbiter (#190) — are affected identically to operational organs. This suggests a systemic issue in Spine's health-check mechanism rather than individual organ failures. The missed_pong events fire in rapid sequence (all within ~0.3 seconds), indicating a synchronized timeout rather than independent organ failures.

SafeVault backups show a pattern of failures and partial completions: May 3 backup hung and was killed (sqlite3 .backup blocked by zombie processes from prior day still alive after ~25h), April 30 backup FAILED entirely due to unbound variable bug (ORGAN_SQLITE_DBS array), April 28/27 had NAS unavailable errors, April 5 failed due to pipefail in git sync step. The May 3 incident reveals zombie backup processes persisting across days and blocking subsequent runs. NAS mount has been consistently failing since at least April 27. S3 backups are completing but NAS (the local network destination) is unavailable, reducing data durability to a single offsite destination.

Radiant stats show scheduled dream operations are disabled: 'scheduled: disabled', 'last_dream: null', 'health: never_run'. Dream cycles have been executed manually (e.g., Dream Cycle #15 on April 12 via skl-Radiant-dreamer, Dream Cycle #13 Phase 1 via bash script), but the autonomous scheduled mechanism is not operational. Memory consolidation (Phase 2) requires LLM-driven processing and is noted as pending/deferred in several cycle reports. Without scheduled dreams, context freshness degrades and memory consolidation does not occur autonomously.

The Hippocampus organ (conversation memory) shows an empty recent_conversations array. Per the architectural vision documented in Radiant memory, Hippocampus is one of the five Monad legs (alongside Radiant, Graphheight, Minder, and Soul) and is responsible for conversation memory. Its emptiness suggests either the organ is not receiving data or it is not yet fully implemented beyond scaffold. Hippocampus appears in spine_state and is cycling ALIVE/DEGRADED with other organs, indicating the process exists but may not be functionally integrated.

raw_responses:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "id": "gap-dream-autonomy-001",
      "description": "The automatic dream cycle (Phase 1 context freshness and Phase 2 memory consolidation) is effectively non-operational. Radiant health metrics show 'never_run', last_dream is null, and the scheduled task is disabled. Phase 2 repeatedly requires manual interactive Claude Code sessions, preventing the DIO from independently maintaining a coherent, consolidated memory. Without autonomous consolidation, context blocks accumulate (16 active, but only 5 expiring within 48h suggests possible TTL over-length or ineffective pruning) and memory bloat risks degrading the precision of Nomos evaluations.",
      "grounded_in": [
        "MSP § Purpose: The MSP translates the BoR into machine-actionable objectives and invariants that Nomos evaluates. A decaying or unconsolidated memory layer directly undermines Nomos' ability to correctly adjudicate Action Proposals, as the operational picture upon which rules rely becomes stale or contradictory.",
        "MSP § Rules: Currently empty (default-permissive), but the absence of automated memory hygiene rules leaves the DIO vulnerable to drift that no automated governance mechanism can detect or correct."
      ],
      "priority": "critical",
      "impact": "high",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "recommended_attention": "The DIO lacks an autonomous nervous system for memory. Without a reliable scheduled dream cycle, all higher-order governance (Nomos, Arbiter) operates on potentially degraded or duplicated information."
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-embedding-coverage-002",
      "description": "Radiant's memory embedding coverage is only 72% (69 with embeddings, 27 without). Semantic similarity and clustering during Phase 2 consolidation rely on embeddings; missing embeddings significantly weaken the dreamer's ability to detect duplicate or related memories, leading to incomplete deduplication and fragmented institutional knowledge.",
      "grounded_in": [
        "MSP § Genome References: Although genome documents do not yet exist, any future genome rules that depend on accurate, deduplicated memory (e.g., event threshold constraints, authorized sequence patterns) will be brittle if 28% of memory entries lack semantic fingerprints.",
        "BoR Article I (Scope of Enterprise): While undefined, the implicit scope includes reliable self-operation. Embedding gaps threaten the DIO's capacity to meet even minimal internal operational standards."
      ],
      "priority": "high",
      "impact": "medium",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "recommended_attention": "Address the embedding generation pipeline: determine why 27 memory entries were stored without embeddings, and ensure future entries are consistently embedded to enable lossless semantic consolidation."
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-backup-impact-memory-003",
      "description": "Frequent backup failures (hung SQLite processes, NAS unavailability, script bugs) have secondary effects on memory/context integrity. When SafeVault fails, the backup of Radiant's SQLite spine is incomplete; in a catastrophic restore scenario, recent context and memory could be lost or corrupted. The 2026-05-03 incident left zombie backup processes contending with the live spine database, risking write contention and silent data loss.",
      "grounded_in": [
        "MSP § Revision History and Amendment Protocol: The MSP relies on append-only, hash-verified versioning. A restore from a corrupted backup could introduce hash mismatches or truncation in the audit trail of msp_version concepts, violating the deterministic integrity expected by Nomos and the Senate.",
        "MSP § Relationship to the Bill of Rights: The MSP is subordinate to the BoR; if a restore silently alters historical state, it may mask prior violations of BoR-derived invariants."
      ],
      "priority": "medium",
      "impact": "high",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "low",
      "recommended_attention": "Hardening the backup pipeline is not directly a memory formation issue, but its fragility directly threatens the long-term durability of the memory system that the MSP's operational constitution depends upon."
    }
  ]
}
